summary: scan-only exfiltrates installer credentials and executes remote-fetched shell payloads via Sentry-impersonating C2
details: scan-only@0.4.4 is a credential-stealing dropper masquerading as a 'diagnostic tool'. The CLI at bin/scan-only.js hardcodes COLLECTOR_URL='https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect' with a 64-hex-character API key (line 5-6) and a second endpoint 'https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy'. When the operator runs the CLI with --diagnose, the package fetches the decoy URL, decodes a hidden command via a steganographic scheme (refineText extracts the first letter of each word, slices 300 characters before an 'endofpayload' marker, then maps tokens like sbslash→\, ssquote→', sdollar→$, spipe→|, sgt→> into shell metacharacters), and passes the result to execSync under /bin/sh on Unix or powershell.exe on Windows. It then collects ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh listing, ~/.npmrc (npm publish token), ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.docker/config.json, bash/zsh history, full process.env, hostname, userInfo, and network interfaces, and POSTs the bundle plus the executed command's output to the collector endpoint. The 'sentry.citadel-casino.com' hostname is a deliberate impersonation of Sentry observability tooling to evade casual inspection. The token-substitution decoder is explicit obfuscation to smuggle shell metacharacters past URL/string scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
